rows.evidence_statement: UniProt identifies human XCL1/lymphotactin as a C motif chemokine ligand, establishing the sequence target and biological context.
rows.grammar_role: Target identity for the metamorphic state-switch dossier.
rows.provenance_notes: External annotation/literature/state evidence only; not a PDB contact table, not an internal runtime report, not native-metric selection, no MD.

rows.evidence_statement: The chemokine-like monomeric XCL1/Ltn10 state is the XCR1 receptor-binding chemokine state.
rows.grammar_role: State A grammar: chemokine-like monomer receptor-binding state.
rows.provenance_notes: External annotation/literature/state evidence only; not a PDB contact table, not an internal runtime report, not native-metric selection, no MD.
rows.source_name: XCL1/Ltn10 chemokine-like monomer XCR1 receptor-binding state
rows.source_url_or_citation: Tuinstra RL et al. Interconversion between two unrelated protein folds in the lymphotactin native state. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A. 2008;105:5057-5062.

rows.evidence_statement: The beta-sandwich dimeric XCL1/Ltn40 state is described as a distinct biologically relevant state with glycosaminoglycan-binding/HIV-inhibitory context.
rows.grammar_role: State B grammar: beta-sandwich dimer state with distinct function.
rows.provenance_notes: External annotation/literature/state evidence only; not a PDB contact table, not an internal runtime report, not native-metric selection, no MD.
rows.source_url_or_citation: Tuinstra RL et al. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A. 2008;105:5057-5062; Dishman AF and Volkman BF. Curr Opin Struct Biol. 2018;50:90-98.

rows.evidence_statement: Because XCL1 interconverts between two unrelated native-state folds with different biological roles, V36 forbids pooling state-A and state-B evidence into one single-fold grammar.
rows.grammar_role: State-switch grammar firewall: do not mix incompatible state evidence.
rows.provenance_notes: External annotation/literature/state evidence only; not a PDB contact table, not an internal runtime report, not native-metric selection, no MD.
